title: [v4.9-rhel] Backport of healthcheck timers
body:
Addresses: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/ACCELFIX-263,
https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-50506, and
https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-50507

Backports:  https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22589 and
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22895

comments.body: Thanks @Luap99 I'd a bit of merge mayhem with that particular file and it looks like I chose badly.  Will add those back.

I also had changed the value passed into the `--healthcheck` option as part of the merge.  I think that's messing things up too.  I have reverted that and hopefully that is the last missing puzzle piece.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I don't know what's broken here, but it looks real.

Hint: you can save yourself hours of CI time by running:
```console
$ hack/bats 220:'podman healthcheck$' 
```
(the dollar sign means "only tests that match 'podman healthcheck<ENDOFSTRING>', because all other tests in this file also include 'podman healthcheck' and there's no need to run them.)

@edsantiago thanks for the pro-tip reminder on running with hack.  I had done that a long time ago, but completely forgot about it.  Will have to see if I can make a better note.

The change you suggested seems to have gotten it through the section that was failing, but now in my sandbox it looks to be failing later when the test is trying to force an error, but it's completing.  I'm hoping it's either my tired eyes missing it, or something messed up in my environment.  I've made your correction, and few nitty comment changes.  Fingers crossed.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.9-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon care to push the shiny, but currently red, button on this one?  Please see @Luap99 's comments above
